title: Reuse a helper from another workspace root (multi-root / @path)
task: Fix `articlePath` in packages/api/src/routes.js so it slugifies the title (lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs collapsed to single hyphens, trimmed) before building the path. Reuse the existing slugify helper from the utils root at packages/utils/src/slugify.js instead of writing a new one. Run `npm test` to verify.
notes: Multi-root scenario: the fix in the `api` root must reuse code in the `utils` root, so the agent has to navigate across roots (the @path / --add-dir use case). The task names paths under packages/api, which also triggers the path-gated subdir rules file packages/api/AGENTS.md (see collectProjectRules in packages/core/src/agent/rules.js) that forbids re-implementing utilities. file_not_contains toLowerCase pins the reuse: a locally re-implemented slugify would lowercase inline and fail. For a literal multi-root CLI run, point one root at packages/api and add the other with --add-dir packages/utils.
